query: 2026 Venezuela earthquakes June 24 confirmed death toll latest figures, trajectory of confirmed deaths reported by authorities and Wikipedia, number of missing persons and rescue operation status
answer:
I've completed the research on the 2026 Venezuela earthquakes. Here's the key synthesis:

## Summary

**The event:** An earthquake doublet on 24 June 2026 — a Mw 7.2 foreshock followed 39 seconds later by a Mw 7.5 mainshock, epicenters in Veroes, Yaracuy. La Guaira and Caracas were hardest hit. It was Venezuela's strongest earthquake since 1900.

**Confirmed death toll trajectory (Venezuelan authorities / Jorge Rodríguez):**
- June 24: ~235
- June 28: 1,450
- June 29: 1,719
- June 30: 1,943
- **July 1: 2,295 (latest confirmed)** — Wikipedia states "more than 2,290 killed, over 11,200 injured"

**Estimated vs. confirmed:** USGS PAGER projected the eventual toll could exceed 100,000 (early range 10,000–100,000, later revised toward >10,000). This is a projection, not a count.

**Missing persons:** The UN and IRC estimate up to ~50,000 may still be missing/trapped. A crowdsourced platform listed 42,655 unaccounted for (June 30). Wikipedia says "tens of thousands."

**Rescue status (as of June 30–July 1):**
- 6,461 rescued alive; ~3,300+ foreign rescuers from 27 countries under UN coordination
- The critical survivor window closed ~June 28; operation entered its "most difficult phase"
- UN began purchasing 10,000 body bags, signaling a shift toward recovery
- Late miracle rescues continued (e.g., a 3-year-old pulled out after 6 days)

**Caveat:** Official government figures are contested by NGO Provea, and there's a large gap between confirmed deaths (~2,300) and estimated missing (~50,000), so the confirmed toll's trajectory was still rising steeply one week out.

Full structured findings (key facts, sources, and data points) have been recorded.

sources.snippet: Death toll 1,943, 10,571 injured (June 30); 6,461 rescued alive; 42,655 still listed missing on platform; 3,300+ rescuers from 27 countries; IRC warns ~50,000 may still be trapped/missing.

sources.snippet: June 29: death toll 1,450, 3,150 injured, 70,000 still missing; 2,741 international rescuers; La Guaira most affected; 20+ countries sent aid.

sources.snippet: Death toll rose to 1,943, 10,571 injured, 28,380 receiving care (June 30); UNICEF estimates ~1.8 million people (680,000 children) need aid.

sources.snippet: At least 188 killed, 1,520 injured; USGS says casualties could range 10,000 to 100,000; at least 200 trapped.

sources.snippet: More than 1,700 people confirmed dead since the earthquakes; miraculous rescues continuing.

sources.snippet: Authorities confirmed at least 1,719 dead; UN coordinating humanitarian assistance; recovery 'going to take time.'

sources.title: Novo balanco. Vitimas mortais dos sismos na Venezuela sobem para 1.943
sources.snippet: 1,943 dead, 10,571 injured; 6,461 rescued; 189 buildings destroyed of 855 damaged; NASA suggests up to 58,870 buildings damaged; UN reports 50,000+ missing.

sources.title: Ascienden a 18 los espanoles fallecidos y a 144 los desaparecidos
sources.snippet: Spanish deaths rose to 18, missing to 144; Venezuela confirmed at least 1,719 dead.

key_facts: Two earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026: a Mw 7.2 foreshock at 18:04 VET followed 39 seconds later by a Mw 7.5 mainshock; epicenters in Veroes, Yaracuy (near Yumare, ~200 km from Caracas).
Latest confirmed death toll is 2,295 (announced July 1, 2026 by National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez); Wikipedia states 'more than 2,290 killed, over 11,200 injured.'
Death toll trajectory: ~235 (June 24) -> 1,450 (June 28) -> 1,719 (June 29) -> 1,943 (June 30) -> 2,295 (July 1); +352 deaths in the single day to July 1.

USGS PAGER predicted the eventual toll could significantly exceed the confirmed total, potentially exceeding 100,000; early Al Jazeera reporting cited a 10,000-100,000 range, later revised toward >10,000 by June 29.
The UN and International Rescue Committee estimate up to ~50,000 people may still be missing/trapped, mostly in La Guaira and Caracas.
Crowdsourced 'Desaparecidos Terremoto Venezuela' platform listed 42,655 still unaccounted for as of June 30 (15,097 of 57,752 reported missing had been found).
6,461 people had been rescued alive as of June 30, 2026.
About 3,300+ foreign rescuers from 27 countries plus 140 search dogs operated under UN coordination.
The critical survivor-finding window closed late Sunday June 28; by June 29-30 the search entered its most difficult recovery phase, with the UN purchasing 10,000 body bags.
La Guaira was the hardest-hit state (Los Corales, Caribe, Caraballeda sectors); 189 buildings fully collapsed, 666-855 partially damaged; NASA estimated up to 58,870 buildings damaged/destroyed.
Over 600 aftershocks occurred, including a 4.6 magnitude aftershock on June 29, causing no significant new damage.

Humanitarian impact: ~15,866 displaced, 22,619-28,380 receiving medical care; UNICEF estimated ~1.8 million people (incl. 680,000 children) need aid; UN estimated up to ~7 million affected and ~$6.7 billion damage (~6% of GDP); ~1.2 million tons of debris.
Foreign national casualties included Spain (18 dead, 144 missing) and Portugal (at least 56 dead, 91 missing/unreachable).
Official government figures were contested by NGO Provea, which called for independent verification, amid public criticism over slow response and lack of heavy machinery.
The mainshock was the strongest earthquake in Venezuela since the 1900 San Narciso earthquake.

data_points.metric: USGS PAGER potential death toll estimate (upper bound)
UN estimate of people still missing
People listed as still unaccounted for (Desaparecidos platform)
